note: Sprint 5.18: adds Trinity Useful Compute human broadcast guard v0.1. New scripts/trinity/useful_compute_broadcast_guard.py is the first Trinity script allowed to broadcast a SOST transaction, but only after the operator passes the exact verbatim token I_UNDERSTAND_THIS_WILL_BROADCAST_A_SIGNED_TRANSACTION. Two modes: --mode local-dry-run (default, NO subprocess, receipt with broadcast_performed=false) and --mode human-broadcast (validates the Sprint 5.17 v0.2 draft, then invokes sost-cli sendrawtransaction <signed_tx_hex> via subprocess.run shell=False). New sost-cli sendrawtransaction <hex> subcommand: thin wrapper around the existing sendrawtransaction RPC (no wallet load, no key handling, no UTXO mutation). Guard refuses the draft if real_signed!=true, signed_tx_hex empty/non-hex, txid_if_signed not 64-hex, signing_scope unknown, capsule_attached!=false, or any of safety_status no_broadcast / human_review_required / requires_separate_broadcast / automatic_payout / private_keys_exported is wrong; refuses if total_payment_stocks > --max-total-stocks BEFORE any subprocess. Also refuses if node-returned txid != draft.txid_if_signed. Receipt v0.1 has 7 const-locked safety flags (human_broadcast_only, requires_manual_confirmation, no_private_keys, no_wallet_access, no_signing, no_automatic_payout, single_transaction_only). Web console adds 'Human Broadcast Guard' card with receipt JSON loader; no fetch/XHR/WebSocket, no wallet/seed/RPC inputs. Static safety: subprocess.run shell=False only, no Popen/call/os.system, no network imports, no wallet/signing tokens, no eval/exec. No consensus, RPC server, node, miner or wire-format changes beyond the new CLI subcommand.
